message:
feat(ftui-text): add incremental Knuth-Plass line-break optimizer (bd-2vr05.15.2.3)

Paragraph-level caching with dirty-region tracking so only edited or
resized paragraphs run the Knuth-Plass DP. Solutions are keyed by
(text_hash, width) for staleness detection. Supports edit notifications,
width/objective changes, selective invalidation, and diagnostic snapshots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","title":"task(test): add advanced API compatibility harness (unit + browser E2E + structured diff logs)","description":"Background
- New API parity work must be proven with explicit compatibility evidence, not only implementation confidence.

Goal
- Build a comprehensive compatibility harness that validates advanced host API behavior against xterm.js reference expectations and FrankenTerm invariants.

Scope
- Unit-level contract tests for event ordering, parser hooks, markers/decorations, and runtime options.
- Browser E2E matrix across key supported engines with deterministic fixtures.
- Structured logging pipeline that captures API-level traces for diffing/replay.
- CI integration as release-blocking checks.

Why This Matters
- Ensures API parity claims are verifiable and regression-resistant.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task is itself the primary validation gate and must include broad unit + E2E coverage.
- E2E harness must include deliberate fault injection and degraded-capability scenarios.
- Structured logs must support side-by-side behavior diffing and triage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compatibility harness covers all advanced API subsystems with passing deterministic fixtures.
- CI executes harness as a release-blocking gate.
- Structured logs include sequence IDs, hook traces, option diffs, and failure diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.658302475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:21:33.153311208Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","ci","e2e","frankenterm","parity","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:13.334234126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.658302475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:11.749740191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:12.155164286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:12.557400483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:12.952311560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","title":"task(docs): publish advanced API migration/capability mapping and troubleshooting guide","description":"Background
- Adoption requires clear migration semantics for advanced APIs, not only base terminal operations.

Goal
- Produce a migration and capability mapping for advanced host APIs from xterm.js concepts to FrankenTermJS primitives.

Scope
- Map key xterm.js advanced APIs/events to FrankenTermJS equivalents and intentional divergences.
- Document capability flags, security constraints, and deterministic guarantees.
- Include troubleshooting guidance tied to structured logs from compatibility harness.

Why This Matters
- Reduces migration friction and support load while preserving intentional architectural differences.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Include doc-tests or schema checks ensuring documented API signatures match shipped types.
- Validate examples via E2E smoke scripts in CI.
- Ensure documentation references structured log fields used during debugging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Migration guide is complete for advanced API parity scope with explicit compatibility table.
- Examples compile/run in CI and match current API signatures.
- Logging/troubleshooting sections are actionable and aligned with implemented telemetry.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:47.059403237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:23:13.299539869Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","docs","frankenterm","migration","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:47.059403237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:14.125633722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): addon-parity closure (fit, web-links, image, progress, ligatures, web-fonts)","description":"Background\n- xterm.js adoption in production often depends on addon capabilities beyond the base terminal surface.\n- Current FrankenTermJS planning covers core remote mode, scrollback/search, rendering, SDK, and conformance, but does not explicitly track parity for fit/web-links/image/progress/ligatures/web-fonts as first-class deliverables.\n\nGoal\n- Close the remaining addon-class capability gap with deterministic, first-party FrankenTermJS modules (not plugin baggage), while preserving bounded-resource and standards-first architecture.\n\nIn Scope\n- Fit-to-container semantics and robust resize observation (fit parity).\n- Hyperlink detection/provider APIs and secure activation UX (web-links parity).\n- Inline image protocol support with bounded decode/render policy (image parity).\n- Progress signaling/event API semantics (progress parity).\n- Optional ligature/shaping rendering path with deterministic fallback (ligatures parity).\n- Web-font lifecycle + metric invalidation controls (web-fonts parity).\n\nOut of Scope\n- Recreating xterm.js addon/plugin architecture as-is.\n- Unbounded buffers/memory for rich media or layout adaptation.\n\nWhy This Matters\n- This closes practical migration blockers for real-world xterm.js users and ensures FrankenTermJS can credibly be a complete replacement, not just a fast core renderer.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- All addon-parity capabilities in scope are implemented with explicit deterministic contracts and capability flags.\n- Unit tests cover core invariants and edge cases for each capability area (fit, links, image, progress, ligatures, web-font lifecycle).\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts cover representative user workflows and emit detailed structured logs (JSONL) with trace/correlation metadata.\n- CI release gates fail on regressions in addon-parity correctness, determinism, or bounded-resource constraints.\n- Migration/readiness artifacts explicitly map these capabilities to xterm.js parity scorecard entries.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this feature.\n- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for materially affected workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Treat each addon-parity capability as a first-party module behind a stable API; avoid plugin-system debt.\n- Ensure every capability has both correctness tests and stress/failure diagnostics with structured logs.\n- Prioritize deterministic behavior and bounded resource usage over opportunistic best-effort rendering.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:12.895368950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:19.255137851Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["addons","fit","frankenterm","image","ligatures","links","parity","progress","wasm","web-fonts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:12.895368950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","title":"task(layout): implement deterministic fit-to-container and web-font metric lifecycle","description":"Background\n- xterm.js users expect reliable fit behavior plus font-loading integration without cursor drift or geometry jumps.\n- FrankenTermJS currently lacks an explicit bead for this parity surface.\n\nScope\n- Implement deterministic fit-to-container API with host callbacks/resize observation contract.\n- Define font-load invalidation pipeline: metric recompute, cache invalidation, patch regeneration boundaries, and throttling.\n- Handle DPR/zoom/container resizes without off-by-one geometry drift.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent resize/font-load event streams yield identical viewport + cursor geometry outcomes.\n- No unbounded resize queue growth under storm conditions.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Fit API behavior is deterministic for fixed input streams (resize, font-load, DPR changes).\n- Unit tests cover metric recompute ordering, cache invalidation, and queue-throttling edge cases.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate resize storms, zoom transitions, and delayed web-font loads with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- Geometry drift, cursor misalignment, and stale-glyph artifacts are regression-gated in CI.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep metric and fit logic in deterministic Rust/WASM state; JS host should remain thin.\n- Explicitly document rounding/tie-break policies for fractional geometry to avoid browser divergence.\n- Add failure diagnostics for font-load race windows and resize burst coalescing decisions.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:42:57.930770004Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fit","frankenterm","layout","renderer","wasm","web-fonts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:44.295223338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:43.930817274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","title":"task(layout): implement deterministic fit-to-container and web-font metric lifecycle","description":"Background\n- xterm.js users expect reliable fit behavior plus font-loading integration without cursor drift or geometry jumps.\n- FrankenTermJS currently lacks an explicit bead for this parity surface.\n\nScope\n- Implement deterministic fit-to-container API with host callbacks/resize observation contract.\n- Define font-load invalidation pipeline: metric recompute, cache invalidation, patch regeneration boundaries, and throttling.\n- Handle DPR/zoom/container resizes without off-by-one geometry drift.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent resize/font-load event streams yield identical viewport + cursor geometry outcomes.\n- No unbounded resize queue growth under storm conditions.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Fit API behavior is deterministic for fixed input streams (resize, font-load, DPR changes).\n- Unit tests cover metric recompute ordering, cache invalidation, and queue-throttling edge cases.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate resize storms, zoom transitions, and delayed web-font loads with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- Geometry drift, cursor misalignment, and stale-glyph artifacts are regression-gated in CI.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep metric and fit logic in deterministic Rust/WASM state; JS host should remain thin.\n- Explicitly document rounding/tie-break policies for fractional geometry to avoid browser divergence.\n- Add failure diagnostics for font-load race windows and resize burst coalescing decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:14.651803885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:14.651785019Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fit","frankenterm","layout","renderer","wasm","web-fonts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:44.295223338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:43.930817274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","title":"task(links): implement OSC-8 + URL-provider hyperlink detection and annotation pipeline","description":"Background\n- xterm.js web-links usage depends on both OSC-8 hyperlinks and plain-text URL detection/provider behavior.\n- FrankenTermJS needs deterministic hyperlink discovery and annotation across viewport + scrollback.\n\nScope\n- Preserve OSC-8 hyperlink metadata through parser/state/grid/scrollback paths.\n- Implement deterministic URL detection/provider API with explicit ordering and conflict policy versus OSC-8.\n- Expose hyperlink annotation data for hover, keyboard navigation, and secure activation layers.\n\nAcceptance\n- Hyperlink ranges remain stable under scroll, resize, and reflow operations.\n- Detection conflicts resolve deterministically with documented precedence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- OSC-8 and URL-provider detection semantics are deterministic and documented (precedence, overlap, invalidation).\n- Unit tests cover hyperlink range stability under unicode graphemes, wraps, scrollback trims, and viewport reflow.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate hover/focus annotation correctness with structured JSONL logs.\n- Differential checks versus xterm.js hyperlink fixtures are wired into CI for covered scenarios.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep link annotations in core deterministic state so selection/copy/search can consume the same canonical ranges.\n- Explicitly log overlap-resolution decisions (OSC-8 vs provider URL) for postmortem clarity.\n- Do not allow hyperlink metadata paths to introduce unbounded per-cell payload growth.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:52.228283813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:46.637087410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","links","parser","security","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:46.637014814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:04.522840714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:52.228283813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:04.879962033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:05.245751779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","title":"task(image): implement bounded inline-image protocol decoding and cell-anchored rendering","description":"Background\n- xterm.js image addon users rely on inline image protocols for modern terminal workflows.\n- FrankenTermJS needs first-party, policy-driven support with hard resource bounds.\n\nScope\n- Parse and validate selected inline image protocol envelopes (capability-gated).\n- Implement deterministic image decode/upload pipeline with strict memory/frame budgets and eviction policy.\n- Render cell-anchored images with correct scroll/resize/viewport behavior and fallback semantics when capabilities are unavailable.\n\nAcceptance\n- Image rendering remains deterministic for fixed inputs/capability profiles.\n- Decode/render paths enforce bounded memory and queue usage under adversarial input.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Protocol parsing/validation for supported inline-image formats is deterministic, capability-gated, and fuzz/regression tested.\n- Unit tests cover decode limits, eviction policy, anchor semantics, and resize/scroll interactions.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate image-heavy workloads and failure modes (oversized payloads, malformed metadata) with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- CI gates fail on budget regressions (memory/frame time) or semantic drift in image positioning behavior.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep all image protocol support explicitly capability-gated and policy-controlled by defaults.\n- Enforce hard decode/upload caps before allocation; fail deterministically with reason-coded diagnostics.\n- Ensure image rendering never bypasses one-writer/frame-budget invariants.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:12.087634054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.371562799Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","image","protocol","renderer","security","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.010551183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:12.087634054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:24.645156728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.371523095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","title":"task(progress): implement deterministic progress signaling API and OSC-mapped semantics","description":"Background\n- xterm.js progress addon usage depends on exposing terminal-originated progress signals to host UI.\n- FrankenTermJS should provide a first-party deterministic progress event surface without addon coupling.\n\nScope\n- Define supported progress signal sources (including mapped escape-sequence forms) and normalization rules.\n- Expose typed progress events with ordering guarantees and bounded delivery semantics.\n- Integrate with host API docs and capability flags.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent input/replay streams produce identical progress event sequences.\n- Unsupported/prohibited signals fail deterministically with traceable diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Progress signal taxonomy and normalization rules are explicit, deterministic, and versioned.\n- Unit tests cover sequence ordering, dedupe/coalescing policy, invalid signal handling, and capability gating.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate host-visible progress lifecycle behavior with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- API contract tests ensure typed event payload stability and error semantics.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep progress events in the same deterministic ordering model as other host events.\n- Do not leak unbounded high-frequency progress updates; enforce bounded delivery/coalescing policy.\n- Include trace IDs linking parsed escape input to emitted host events.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:31.771885449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:02.228948627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","parser","progress","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:42.364874191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:42.787264441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:43.211698509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:31.771885449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1043,7 +1043,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","title":"feature(text-layout): TeX-class paragraph breaking, leading, and justification (incremental + deterministic)","description":"Goal\n- Implement a real-time paragraph layout core that delivers TeX-class text quality while preserving deterministic behavior and frame budgets.\n\nScope\n- Explicit quality objective (badness/demerits) and constraint system.\n- Incremental Knuth-Plass style line breaking suitable for live edits/resizes.\n- Leading/baseline-grid model and paragraph spacing policy.\n- Hyphenation and penalty tuning with deterministic dictionaries/configuration.\n- Policy presets and fallback modes for constrained environments.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Paragraph layout quality objective is documented, implemented, and test-covered.\n- Incremental layout updates avoid full-paragraph recomputation for local edits/resizes.\n- Justified output quality is measurably improved versus greedy baseline while preserving determinism.\n- Unit/property tests and browser/fixture E2E scenarios validate correctness under multilingual and edge-case inputs.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Separate semantic correctness from quality optimization so fallbacks preserve meaning/cursor semantics even when quality tier drops.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":1500,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:40.084962777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:40.572584502Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hyphenation","justification","layout","linebreak","text","typography"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:40.084962777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.1","title":"task(layout-core): define formal paragraph objective (badness/demerits/constraints) and invariants","description":"Specify objective function and constraints for line-breaking/justification from first principles, including penalties for rivers, widow/orphan lines, stretch/compress bounds, and readability guards.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Objective function and invariants are explicit and implementable.\n2) Golden examples demonstrate expected decision outcomes.\n3) Unit tests validate cost component monotonicity and deterministic tie-breaks.","notes":"This is the contract that prevents heuristic drift and hidden quality regressions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:55.552648324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:36:45.142835767Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:36:45.142806021Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","math","typography"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:55.552648324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1184,"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LilacBison: Implemented formal paragraph objective in ftui-text/src/wrap.rs. Added FitnessClass, BreakKind, BreakPenalty, ParagraphObjective with badness/demerits/adjacency/widow/orphan methods. terminal() and typographic() presets. 24 unit tests, all 674 ftui-text tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:36:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.2","title":"task(layout-core): implement deterministic hyphenation + penalty model","description":"Implement deterministic hyphenation data path and penalty model compatible with incremental line breaking and multilingual text handling.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Hyphenation decisions are reproducible for fixed dictionaries/policies.\n2) Unit tests cover ambiguous and edge-case words, including no-hyphen fallback.\n3) E2E fixtures verify visible impact on justified paragraphs without semantic corruption.","notes":"Design for optional dictionaries and bounded memory footprint.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:56.522079602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:44:59.753223516Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:44:59.753202877Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hyphenation","language","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:56.522079602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:01.971672181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1185,"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LilacBison: Implemented Liang's TeX hyphenation algorithm in ftui-text/src/hyphenation.rs. PatternTrie for fast lookup, HyphenationDict with patterns + exceptions, configurable margins, penalty integration with BreakPenalty. Mini English pattern set (100+ patterns). 36 tests, all 710 ftui-text tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:44:55Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","title":"task(layout-core): implement incremental Knuth-Plass style line-break optimizer","description":"Build an incremental line-break optimizer that updates affected regions only during edits/resizes, preserving deterministic output and bounded latency.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Local edits/resizes trigger bounded relayout scope with correctness equivalence to full recompute.\n2) Deterministic output for fixed text+policy+geometry inputs.\n3) Property tests validate no-overflow, stable ordering, and idempotence under repeated recompute.","notes":"Must interoperate cleanly with resize arbiter decisions from web-rescale track.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":330,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:57.501308677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:03.400258235Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incremental","knuth-plass","paragraph","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:03.400197321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:57.501308677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:02.453981147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:02.953217663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","title":"task(layout-core): implement incremental Knuth-Plass style line-break optimizer","description":"Build an incremental line-break optimizer that updates affected regions only during edits/resizes, preserving deterministic output and bounded latency.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Local edits/resizes trigger bounded relayout scope with correctness equivalence to full recompute.\n2) Deterministic output for fixed text+policy+geometry inputs.\n3) Property tests validate no-overflow, stable ordering, and idempotence under repeated recompute.","notes":"Must interoperate cleanly with resize arbiter decisions from web-rescale track.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":330,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:57.501308677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:53.944743237Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incremental","knuth-plass","paragraph","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:03.400197321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:57.501308677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:02.453981147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:02.953217663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.4","title":"task(layout-core): implement leading/baseline-grid and paragraph spacing system","description":"Introduce vertical layout model (leading/baseline alignment/paragraph spacing) with deterministic policy tiers and readability constraints.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Baseline and leading calculations are deterministic and documented.\n2) Unit tests cover edge cases (small viewports, mixed styles, high zoom).\n3) Visual snapshots verify stable vertical rhythm across DPI/zoom matrices.","notes":"Vertical rhythm must remain coherent under dynamic reflow.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:58.590331677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:03.829623727Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","leading","typography","vertical-rhythm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:58.590331677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:03.829573473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.5","title":"task(layout-core): add microtypographic justification controls (stretch/shrink/spacing penalties)","description":"Add controlled spacing modulation to improve justified quality while honoring readability and deterministic constraints.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Spacing modulation remains within declared bounds and preserves semantic text content.\n2) Quality metrics improve versus greedy baseline on benchmark corpus.\n3) Tests validate deterministic tie-break behavior and no catastrophic spacing artifacts.","notes":"Keep controls policy-driven and observable for debugging/tuning.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:59.723145704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:04.729347276Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["justification","microtypography","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:59.723145704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:04.266785989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:04.729283226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.6","title":"task(layout-api): define policy presets + fallback contract for quality tiers","description":"Define user-facing policy presets (quality-balanced-fast) and deterministic fallback rules mapping algorithmic features to runtime tiers.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Policy presets are explicit, versioned, and documented.\n2) Fallback contract guarantees semantic correctness when quality features are disabled.\n3) Unit/API contract tests enforce stable behavior across versions.","notes":"This API is the control surface used by degradation controller.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:00.920577937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:06.099656267Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fallback","layout","policy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:00.920577937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:05.153387656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:05.645962316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.15.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:41:06.099601264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
